summary: R-7 prevents the reported crash paths and the CI run is valid, but it is not fully closed: callable values can bypass the field allowlist and leak attached credentials through successful coercion. No critical issue found; one medium security regression remains.

findings.title: Callable values bypass the non-expanding error sanitizer
findings.body: The primitive branch includes functions because `typeof function === "function"`, so it invokes `String(e)` instead of reading only allowlisted fields. A callable carrying `client.password` with a non-throwing `Symbol.toPrimitive` logged that password in both this formatter and worker `src/index.ts:53`. The pre-60e0ec9 DB formatter logged `undefined` for the same value, making this a regression introduced by the db.ts application. Verification tests throwing coercion and a credential-bearing Error separately, so green CI misses this cross-case. A callable emitted or thrown by application or SDK code could therefore expose credentials or PII in centralized logs.
findings.recommendation: Treat functions as object-like and route them through the guarded `code`/`stack`/`message` reads; only coerce actual primitives. Add worker and DB regression cases using a callable with an attached sentinel and a successful `Symbol.toPrimitive`, asserting the hook's value is never logged.

next_steps: Fix both formatter copies and add the callable credential-sentinel regression case.
Re-run the API tests, worker hostile-value container harness, and five-job template CI at the fix SHA; refresh the committed evidence.
